claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation is the most common mutation identified in the Malaysian IOPD cohort, occurring in 8 patients (10 alleles) with an allele frequency of 33%.

citations.quote: The most common mutation identified was c.1935C>A p.(D645E), occurring in eight patients (10 alleles) with allele frequency of (33%).

claims.text: Two unrelated patients (patients 13 and 17) were homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E), and six other patients carried it as compound heterozygotes.

citations.quote: Two unrelated patients were homozygous for the c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation. The other 13 were compound heterozygotes.

claims.text: Patient 5 (Chinese, classic IOPD) carried c.1935C>A p.(D645E) in compound heterozygosity with c.2024_2026delACA p.(N675del); presented at age 4 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; GAA activity not available; deceased at 8 months.

Patient 6 (Chinese, classic IOPD) carried c.1935C>A p.(D645E) compound heterozygous with c.1843G>A p.(G615R); presented at age 6 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; deceased at 10 months.

Patient 7 (Chinese, classic IOPD) carried c.1935C>A p.(D645E) compound heterozygous with c.1411_1414delGAGA p.(E471Pfs*5); presented at age 3 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, and feeding difficulties; GAA activity <0.1 umol/h/L; LVMI 485 g/m2; ejection fraction 9.9%; deceased at 2 years.

Patient 9 (Chinese, classic IOPD) carried c.1935C>A p.(D645E) compound heterozygous with c.1411_1414delGAGA p.(E471Pfs*5); presented at age 3 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; deceased at 3 years.

Patient 12 (Chinese, classic IOPD) carried c.1935C>A p.(D645E) compound heterozygous with c.2024_2026delACA p.(N675del); presented at age 2 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, and feeding difficulties; GAA activity 5.3%; LVMI 159 g/m2; ejection fraction 81.6%; alive at age 5 years 10 months.

Patient 13 (Chinese, classic IOPD) was homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E); presented at age 4 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; GAA activity 2.0%; LVMI 483 g/m2; ejection fraction 28%; deceased at 2 years.

Patient 15 (Chinese, classic IOPD) carried c.1935C>A p.(D645E) compound heterozygous with c.2662G>T p.(E888*); presented at age 4 months; GAA activity 0.6%; LVMI 461 g/m2; ejection fraction 33%; deceased at 7 months.

Patient 17 (Malay, classic IOPD) was homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E); presented at age 2 months with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, respiratory insufficiency, hypotonia, feeding difficulties, and hepatomegaly; GAA activity 1.7%; ejection fraction 29%; deceased at 6 months.
The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation has been reported to cause both IOPD and LOPD, and is the most common mutation in Southern China (25%), Taiwan (36%), and Thailand (32.4%).

citations.quote: This mutation has been reported to cause both IOPD and LOPD [28, 29]. The Chinese patients in Malaysia share other common mutations with patients from China and Taiwan

claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation was identified as the most common mutation in this Malaysian IOPD cohort and is established as a founder effect mutation in the Chinese population.

citations.quote: This is the first study that analyzes the genotype and phenotype of IOPD patients in Malaysia, and has established the c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation as the most common mutation, with an allele frequency of 33%.

claims.text: Four patients in the cohort sharing c.1935C>A p.(D645E) (two homozygous, two compound heterozygous with c.1411_1414del p.(E471Pfs*5)) all died between 6 months and 3 years without achieving motor milestones, in contrast to similarly genotyped Taiwanese patients diagnosed by newborn screening who achieved independent walking.

citations.quote: Four of our patients share similar molecular mutations [two patients were homozygous for c.1935C>A p.(D645E), two patients were compound heterozygous for c.1411_1414del p.(E471Pfs*5) and c.1935C>A p.(D645E)] with patients in a study in Taiwan who were diagnosed via newborn screening and given very early ERT prior to symptom onset

claims.text: The c.1935C>A p.(D645E) mutation is predicted to be CRIM positive based on the Erasmus Pompe database.

citations.quote: CRIM status predicted based on Erasmus Pompe database (http://www.pompevariantdatabase.nl/ updated in Dec 2020)
